overview: Black Sacrifice in Pauper leverages low-cost creatures and sacrifice outlets to generate continuous value and clear opposing boards. By repeatedly sacrificing creatures to Carrion Feeder and Mortician Beetle, you create a recursive loop that outvalues opponents while maintaining combat pressure. Village Rites serves as both efficient removal and a trigger source, ensuring you can bypass blockers and close out games quickly.
win_conditions: Combat damage after clearing blockers with Village Rites
Attrition via repeated Mortician Beetle and Carrion Feeder triggers
Board state advantage from forcing opponent sacrifices

keep_criteria: Hands with turn 1 or 2 creature plays
At least one sacrifice outlet or removal spell
Two or more lands with an early mana curve
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, a sacrifice outlet (Carrion Feeder, Mortician Beetle), and a cantrip/ritual (Village Rites, Dark Ritual, Thoughtseize).
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early creatures or lands
Hands with only high-cost cards or late-game value
Hands without removal or sacrifice triggers
Mulligan if you have 0-1 lands, only high-cost spells, or no way to generate initial value.

early_game: Play cheap creatures to establish board presence
Prioritize casting Carrion Feeder by turn 2
Hold Village Rites for early blockers or to trigger sacrifice effects
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Dark Ritual or Thoughtseize to disrupt.
Turn 2: Play a sacrifice outlet, then sacrifice a 1/1 (like Gravecrawler or token) to draw cards/gain life.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast outlets on curve.
mid_game: Use Village Rites to remove key threats and trigger death abilities
Sacrifice creatures to Carrion Feeder to pump it into a combat threat
Trigger Mortician Beetle recursion to force opponent sacrifices
Balance mana for threats vs. outlets.
Don't overcommit to sacrifice if you need creatures to block or attack.
Keep graveyard stocked but be mindful of hate.
Use Village Rites/Dark Ritual to maintain hand size.
Commit threats when you have a clear advantage in card advantage or life total.
Hold back outlets if the opponent has sweepers or hate.
late_game: Maintain a wide board of pumped creatures
Use sacrifice triggers to drain opponent resources
Push combat damage while opponent lacks answers
Graveyard recursion (Sheoldred, Liliana), relentless value engines (Mortician Beetle + Carrion Feeder), or direct damage from sacrifice outlets.
When you have a clear card advantage lead, opponent is low on interaction, or you can close the game before they can stabilize.

key_cards.name: Carrion Feeder
key_cards.role: Core sacrifice engine and combat threat
key_cards.notes: Sacrifice cheap creatures or opponent's creatures to pump it. Keep it alive as long as possible to maximize value and close games.

key_cards.name: Mortician Beetle
key_cards.notes: Play early to trigger opponent sacrifices. When it dies, it can trigger itself again if you sacrifice another creature, creating a chain reaction.

key_cards.name: Village Rites
key_cards.notes: Use to kill blockers or finish the game. Sacrificing a creature to cast it can trigger multiple death abilities simultaneously.

sequencing_tips: Sacrifice creatures to Carrion Feeder before casting Village Rites to maximize pump
Hold Village Rites in hand to respond to opponent's combat tricks or board wipes
Avoid sacrificing your last creature unless it directly wins the game or triggers a lethal chain
Trigger Mortician Beetle's death ability by sacrificing it to Carrion Feeder or Village Rites
Common mistake: Over-sacrificing too early and running out of resources.
Unique interaction: Carrion Feeder's draw triggers on sacrifice, not death, so it works with tokens and sacrifice outlets.
Always keep a land in hand if you're playing a format with fetch/shock lands to avoid flooding.

matchups.advice: Use Village Rites early to clear aggressive threats. Keep Carrion Feeder alive to outvalue their burn spells.
Play around board wipes by keeping a wide board. Use sacrifice triggers to pressure life total before they stabilize.
Clear their wide boards with Village Rites and Beetle recursion. Pump Feeder to match their combat stats.
Use cheap, multiple threats (Village Rites, Dark Ritual) to force counter windows. Bounce or discard to disrupt their mana. Don't overcommit to one big spell.
Apply early pressure, use discard to hit their lands, and prioritize removal over value generation. Out-grave them if they go wide.
Stabilize with life gain from Mortician Beetle/Village Rites, use removal to clear key threats, and transition to value once stabilized. Don't over-sacrifice early.
Pressure with value engines, use discard to hit their answers, and win through attrition. Force them to use resources on your small threats.
Win the card advantage war. Prioritize discard, protect your outlets, and be willing to trade creatures to maintain graveyard presence.

common_threats: Watch for graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Leyline, Tormod's Crypt), sweepers (Wrath of God, Damnation), and combo wincons.
Note when opponents tap out for big spells vs. holding up interaction.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Derived from signature card interactions and standard Pauper format dynamics.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Based on recursive sacrifice mechanics and low-mana format pacing.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Based on standard sacrifice deck sequencing principles and card text interactions.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Common Pauper format threats that interact with creature-heavy strategies.
